page.title: create workflow
page.description: build a visual workflow with drag-and-drop nodes

statusBanner.pluginMissing: this workflow uses plugins that are no longer installed:

validation.nodesRequired: at least one node is required in the workflow
validation.fixErrors: please fix validation errors before saving
validation.atLeastOneCondition: at least one condition is required
validation.valueSourceIncomplete: value source is incomplete — select a type and provide a value
validation.atLeastOneCase: at least one case is required
validation.caseNeedsCondition: {caseName}: at least one condition is required
validation.loopSourceIncomplete: loop source is incomplete — select a type and provide a value
validation.errorCodeRequired: error code is required when terminating with error
validation.gotoSenderNeedsReceiver: sender with label "{label}" has no matching receiver
validation.gotoReceiverNeedsSender: receiver with label "{label}" has no matching sender
validation.compareToIncomplete: comparison value is incomplete — select a type and provide a value
validation.tightCycleDetected: this node is part of a cycle with no async pause — add a wait, signal, or delay node to break the loop

notifications.invalidWorkflowNodes: invalid workflow: "nodes" must be an array
notifications.invalidWorkflowEdges: invalid workflow: "edges" must be an array

dialogs.unsavedChangesCancel: you have unsaved changes. Are you sure you want to cancel?
dialogs.unsavedChangesLeave: you have unsaved changes. Do you want to leave without saving?

generalTab.name: name *
generalTab.isTemplate: is template (can be reused as base for other flows)
generalTab.timezoneInfo: used for delay, schedule, cron and date comparisons
generalTab.nonRetryableErrorsHint: errors that skip retry and go directly to error output
generalTab.retryInfo: failed nodes will be retried up to {maxAttempts} times with exponential backoff. Non-retryable errors skip retry and go directly to the error output.

variablesTab.inputsHelp: external variables that callers must provide when invoking this workflow
variablesTab.stateHelp: state variables accessible via state.{name} in any node
variablesTab.captureFieldsHelp: fields captured for searches and reports
variablesTab.signalsHelp: external signals that this workflow can wait for using wait_signal nodes

variables.name: name *
variables.descriptionHint: brief description of what this variable is for
variables.ephemeralTooltip: variable resets to its default value at the start of each workflow execution. Ideal for temporary counters, intermediate results, or per-run data.
variables.durableTooltip: variable value persists across workflow executions. The next run starts with the last saved value instead of the default. Ideal for accumulators, totals, or state that must survive across runs.
variables.emptyDescription: state variables persist during workflow execution and are accessible in any node via state.{name}

externalInputs.label: label *
externalInputs.descriptionHint: help text for callers providing this variable
externalInputs.emptyDescription: input variables define the contract for callers invoking this workflow — they appear in subworkflow nodes and API triggers
externalInputs.literalValueHint: this value is embedded in the workflow definition — callers cannot change it
externalInputs.literalInfo: literal inputs are constants defined by the workflow author. The value is fixed and not provided by callers at runtime.
externalInputs.assetTemplateHint: only assets from this template will be available for selection
externalInputs.fieldPathHint: which asset field will be used as the input value
externalInputs.assetFromTemplateInfo: the caller will select an asset from the chosen template. The value is automatically extracted from the selected field.
externalInputs.eventFieldsBannerDescription: nodes already access event data directly via the Event Field source type. Use inputs only for static values or asset selection.

captureFields.emptyDescription: capture fields are stored in ClickHouse for search and reporting

externalSignals.name: signal name *
externalSignals.emptyDescription: signals define the external events this workflow can wait for — they are used by wait_signal nodes and serve as a contract for callers

jsonDebug.title: JSON debug
jsonDebug.descriptionView: inspect the current workflow definition as JSON
jsonDebug.descriptionEdit: edit JSON and apply changes to all tabs
jsonDebug.structureExternalVariables: externalVariables — input contract for callers (field, label, type, required)
jsonDebug.structureNodes: nodes — DAG nodes (id, type, position, config)
jsonDebug.structureEdges: edges — DAG edges (id, source, target, handles)
jsonDebug.editModeHint1: paste a complete workflow JSON to import
jsonDebug.editModeHint2: modify fields directly in the editor
jsonDebug.editModeHint4: apply changes to sync all tabs

nodeConfig.noConfigForm: no config form registered for this node type
nodeConfig.notesInfo: add notes to document this node. When set, an info badge appears on the canvas node.
nodeConfig.notesPlaceholder: describe what this node does, why it's here, or any important context...

canvasToolbar.shortcutsDescription: use these shortcuts to work faster in the workflow editor.

pluginsTab.installedHelp: plugins currently active in this workflow editor
pluginsTab.marketplaceHelp: browse and install workflow plugins from the marketplace
pluginsTab.noPluginsInstalledDesc: browse the marketplace to discover and install plugins
pluginsTab.noPluginsAvailable: no plugins available in the marketplace
pluginsTab.credentialsMockInfo: credentials are stored locally in this phase (mock). Backend integration coming soon.

timeout.enableOutputHint: When enabled, timeout expiry routes to a 'timeout' output instead of failing the execution

credentials.title: credentials — {name}
credentials.credentialNameHint: a label to identify this credential
credentials.noCredentialsDesc: create a credential to authenticate with {name}

errorHandler.banner: Automatically retry the node before following the error output. Independent from timeout — they never interact.
errorHandler.maxAttemptsHint: Maximum number of retry attempts before following the error handle
errorHandler.backoffMultiplierHint: Multiplier applied to the interval after each failed attempt (exponential backoff)
